message.content:
POLARIS mission: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
WORK ONLY IN /home/polaris/wt/compete. NEVER cd into /home/polaris/wt/tooluse or run outline pytest —
that is a DIFFERENT wheel's job. You are NOT here to run unit tests. You are here to SCORE REPORTS.

Reference (read-only): /workspace/POLARIS. Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md
(ONLY ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped, Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped exist — there is NO
FS-Researcher artifact; report that gap, do not fake a third column).
Existing rendered POLARIS reports to score: find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md
Scorer in tree: scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, deeptrace_judge_preflight.py,
config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml. Mission says RACE (DeepResearch Bench:
comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability). DeepTRACE (citation auditing) != RACE.

HONESTY IS THE ENTIRE POINT: if POLARIS loses, say so and by how much. Never tune the judge to win.
Do NOT burn a fresh 346-basket render — score reports that already exist. Uses OpenRouter (shared
account); if you see 429, say so, do not silently retry forever. Never print the API key.


STEP 2 — SCORE. Given what STEP 1 found:
{
  "scorer_exists": true,
  "is_it_RACE": "it_is_DEEPTRACE",
  "scorer_details": "The in-tree, operator-signed scorer is DeepTRACE, NOT RACE. scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py computes the 8 DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness audit metrics (Salesforce arXiv 2509.04499): I One-Sided, II Overconfident, III Relevant-Statements ratio, IV Uncited-Sources fraction, V Unsupported-Statements ratio, VI Source-Necessity (greedy min-set-cover / listed sources), VII Citation-Accuracy = sum(C x S)/sum(C), VIII Citation-Thoroughness = sum(C x S)/sum(S). All operate over a statement x source Citation matrix C and a judge-built Factual-Support matrix S. It is explicitly a RE-IMPLEMENTATION and self-labels is_estimate=True / scorer='polaris-reimpl-of-deeptrace' (no public official DeepTRACE scorer exists). Judge lock (config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml, signed 2026-07-04 by aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com, commit 8e9811bb): benchmark_judge = moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 (substituting the paper's GPT-5, DISCLOSED); comparability.policy = self_rescore (POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor with the SAME judge; absolute numbers disclosed as non-paper; never mix self-scored vs paper-published); provider_headroom floor = 8 operational OpenRouter providers, ~178 statement-support calls/report; deeptrace_judge_preflight.py fails loud if unsigned or below headroom. This measures citation/faithfulness auditing (does each cited source actually support the statement), NOT report quality.",
  "what_mission_needs": "RACE = the DeepResearch Bench (v1) reference-based, adaptive-criteria LLM judge scoring reports on 4 quality dimensions: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, weighted by dynamically-generated per-task criteria against a reference answer. That is a report-QUALITY axis. DeepTRACE is a citation-FAITHFULNESS axis. They are orthogonal — a report can score high on DeepTRACE (every citation supports its claim) yet low on RACE (shallow, ignores the prompt, unreadable), and vice-versa. The mission (beat ChatGPT+Gemini+FS-Researcher on deep-research REPORTS) is a RACE-shaped quality comparison.",
  "scorer_details_note": "n/a",
  "gap": "Large. There is NO RACE scorer anywhere in the tree: grep for comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability as a 4-dim reference-based judge returns nothing that scores reports that way. What exists on the quality axis instead: (1) DeepTRACE (citation auditing, implemented + signed) — wrong axis; (2) scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py — a THIN adapter to the OFFICIAL DeepResearch-Bench-II harness (Gemini rubric judge, three-way 1/0/-1 over 9,415 expert rubric items), but third_party/ does NOT exist in the worktree (no run_evaluation.py, no tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl) and DRB-II is a rubric-item benchmark, not the RACE 4-dimension weighted framework; (3) src/polaris_graph/benchmark/dimension_scorers.py — a bespoke POLARIS 'BEAT-BOTH' 7-dim HEURISTIC scorer (sourcing_tier_mix, numeric_grounding, provenance_density, refusal_correctness, coverage_completeness, latency, auditability) with no LLM judge and no reference answer — POLARIS's own axis, not RACE and not comparable to the paper. So: to satisfy the mission you must either build a RACE scorer (4-dim reference-based judge) or stand up the DRB-II official harness; neither is present/runnable today. Relabeling DeepTRACE as RACE would be dishonest.",
  "recommendation": "ADAPT, do not relabel. Two honest paths: (A) BUILD RACE — implement the DeepResearch Bench RACE 4-dimension reference-based judge (needs reference answers per task + adaptive criteria weighting + an LLM judge), then self-rescore POLARIS AND all 4 competitor artifacts with the same judge/tasks (mirror the self_rescore comparability discipline already codified in the DeepTRACE lock). (B) STAND UP DRB-II — fetch the official third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II harness + tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl and run the Gemini rubric judge (closest to a real DeepResearch-Bench number; the wrapper is already written). Do NOT use DeepTRACE as-is to claim a RACE win — it answers a different question. DeepTRACE can still be reported HONESTLY as a citation-faithfulness result (with the is_estimate / kimi-substitution / self_rescore disclosures), just not as RACE. Watch cost: both A and B are paid OpenRouter/Gemini judge runs; score the ~35 existing rendered reports under /workspace/POLARIS/outputs rather than triggering a fresh 346-basket render, and surface 429s rather than silently retrying.",
  "fsr_missing": true
}

Run the scorer that ACTUALLY exists against EXISTING reports (do not build a fresh render). Score at
least one real POLARIS report (find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md — pick a substantive
recent one) against the 4 competitor artifacts. Produce a REAL head-to-head table per dimension.
If the scorer needs a reference/question to score against, find the matching one in the repo. If you
genuinely cannot run it end-to-end, say exactly why in blockers — do NOT fabricate a table.
Report whether POLARIS wins or loses, per dimension, with margins. A loss reported honestly is the
whole point of this wheel.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 — DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scorer (re-implementation).
2	
3	DeepTRACE (Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499, ICLR 2026) defines 8 audit metrics over a report's
4	statements x sources. There is NO public official scorer/leaderboard, so this is a RE-IMPLEMENTATION whose
5	output is an ESTIMATE, not a proven score: the paper's judge is GPT-5 (Pearson 0.62 vs human); we substitute
6	our D8 kimi-k2.6 judge to build the support matrix (DISCLOSED). Calibrate against the paper's published
7	GPT-5-Deep-Research column before claiming a rank.
8	
9	Inputs (built upstream from the rendered report):
10	  * citation_matrix  C : list[list[int]] of shape [n_statements][n_sources]; C[i][j]=1 iff statement i
11	    cites source j.
12	  * support_matrix   S : list[list[int]] of the same shape; S[i][j]=1 iff source j's content SUPPORTS
13	    statement i (per the judge).
14	  * per-statement metadata: relevant (bool), confidence (int 1-5 or None), stance ('pro'|'con'|'neutral').
15	
16	Exact formulas (paper section on metrics):
17	  III Relevant Statements   = relevant / total
18	  IV  Uncited Sources       = cited / listed           (we also report uncited = 1 - cited/listed)
19	  V   Unsupported Statements = unsupported / relevant   (a relevant statement with no supporting source)
20	  VII Citation Accuracy     = sum(C (x) S) / sum(C)
21	  VIII Citation Thoroughness = sum(C (x) S) / sum(S)
22	  I   One-Sided  (debate)   = 1 iff NOT (>=1 pro AND >=1 con) relevant statement
23	  II  Overconfident (debate)= 1 iff One-Sided AND answer confidence == 5
24	  VI  Source Necessity      = size_of_minimum_source_cover / listed, where the minimum source cover is the
25	      FEWEST sources whose union still SUPPORTS every supported relevant statement (paper 2509.04499
26	      "minimum vertex cover for source nodes"; the official answer-engine-eval reference uses greedy set
27	      cover, which we match — no exact Hopcroft-Karp needed). A statement supported by 2 sources therefore
28	      contributes cover-size 1 (necessity 0.5 of 2 listed), NOT 0. The old SOLE-supporter count (a source
29	      that is the only supporter of some supported relevant statement) is retained as a SECONDARY
30	      disclosure field ``n_sole_supporter`` — it is a lower bound on the cover size, nothing is lost.
31	"""
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	from typing import Any, Optional, Sequence
35	
36	
37	def _sum_hadamard(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]], b: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
38	    """sum(a (x) b) — element-wise product summed over the whole matrix."""
39	    total = 0
40	    for row_a, row_b in zip(a, b):
41	        for x, y in zip(row_a, row_b):
42	            total += int(bool(x)) * int(bool(y))
43	    return total
44	
45	
46	def _sum_matrix(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
47	    return sum(int(bool(x)) for row in a for x in row)
48	
49	
50	def _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]], n_sources: int) -> set[int]:
51	    cited: set[int] = set()
52	    for row in citation_matrix:
53	        for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)):
54	            if row[j]:
55	                cited.add(j)
56	    return cited
57	
58	
59	def necessary_source_count(
60	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
61	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
62	    n_sources: int,
63	) -> int:
64	    """SECONDARY disclosure (NOT the DeepTRACE VI numerator anymore): count sources that are the SOLE
65	    supporter of at least one relevant statement. Such a source must appear in every cover, so this count
66	    is a LOWER BOUND on the minimum source cover size (see ``minimum_source_cover_size``). Redundantly-
67	    supported statements contribute no sole supporter; a statement supported by exactly one source makes
68	    that source a sole supporter. Retained so nothing is lost after the min-cover fix."""
69	    necessary: set[int] = set()
70	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
71	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
72	            continue
73	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
74	        if len(supporters) == 1:
75	            necessary.add(supporters[0])
76	    return len(necessary)
77	
78	
79	def minimum_source_cover_size(
80	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
81	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
82	    n_sources: int,
83	) -> int:
84	    """DeepTRACE VI numerator: the size of the MINIMUM source cover over the supported relevant statements.
85	
86	    universe = indices of relevant statements that have >= 1 supporting source (i.e. supported).
87	    sets     = for each listed source j, the set of supported relevant statements it supports (S[i][j]==1).
88	    The cover = the fewest sources whose union covers the whole universe. Computed by greedy set cover
89	    (repeatedly take the source covering the most still-uncovered statements), which is the standard
90	    reference implementation used by the official answer-engine-eval code — exact Hopcroft-Karp is not
91	    required. Deterministic tie-break: lowest source index. A statement supported by 2 sources yields a
92	    cover of size 1 (not 0), fixing the sole-supporter divergence. Returns 0 for an empty universe."""
93	    universe: set[int] = set()
94	    source_sets: dict[int, set[int]] = {j: set() for j in range(n_sources)}
95	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
96	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
97	            continue
98	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
99	        if not supporters:  # unsupported relevant statement is not in the cover universe
100	            continue
101	        universe.add(i)
102	        for j in supporters:
103	            source_sets[j].add(i)
104	
105	    uncovered = set(universe)
106	    cover_size = 0
107	    while uncovered:
108	        best_j: Optional[int] = None
109	        best_cov = 0
110	        for j in range(n_sources):
111	            cov = len(source_sets[j] & uncovered)
112	            if cov > best_cov:
113	                best_cov = cov
114	                best_j = j
115	        if best_j is None:  # no source covers a remaining statement (unreachable: each has a supporter)
116	            break
117	        uncovered -= source_sets[best_j]
118	        cover_size += 1
119	    return cover_size
120	
121	
122	def _confidence_is_overconfident(
123	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
124	    statement_confidence: Sequence[Optional[int]],
125	) -> bool:
126	    """The answer is confidence-5 if an explicit answer confidence is 5, else if the max per-statement
127	    confidence is 5 (the paper's confidence scoring is statement-level)."""
128	    if answer_confidence is not None:
129	        return int(answer_confidence) == 5
130	    vals = [int(c) for c in statement_confidence if c is not None]
131	    return bool(vals) and max(vals) == 5
132	
133	
134	def compute_deeptrace_metrics(
135	    *,
136	    citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
137	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
138	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
139	    n_sources: int,
140	    stance: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
141	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
142	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
143	    is_debate: bool = False,
144	) -> dict[str, Any]:
145	    """Compute all 8 DeepTRACE metrics. Debate-only metrics (One-Sided, Overconfident) are None when
146	    ``is_debate`` is False. Ratios are 0.0 when their denominator is 0 (an empty answer). PURE / offline."""
147	    n_statements = len(citation_matrix)
148	    total = n_statements
149	    n_relevant = sum(1 for i in range(total) if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
150	
151	    # III Relevant Statements
152	    relevant_ratio = (n_relevant / total) if total else 0.0
153	
154	    # IV Uncited Sources (report cited fraction + its complement)
155	    cited_cols = _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix, n_sources)
156	    cited_fraction = (len(cited_cols) / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
157	    uncited_fraction = (1.0 - cited_fraction) if n_sources else 0.0
158	
159	    # V Unsupported Statements (over RELEVANT statements)
160	    unsupported = 0
161	    for i in range(total):
162	        if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i]:
163	            row = support_matrix[i] if i < len(support_matrix) else []
164	            if not any(row[j] for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources))):
165	                unsupported += 1
166	    unsupported_ratio = (unsupported / n_relevant) if n_relevant else 0.0
167	
168	    # VII / VIII Citation Accuracy / Thoroughness
169	    cs = _sum_hadamard(citation_matrix, support_matrix)
170	    sum_c = _sum_matrix(citation_matrix)
171	    sum_s = _sum_matrix(support_matrix)
172	    citation_accuracy = (cs / sum_c) if sum_c else 0.0
173	    citation_thoroughness = (cs / sum_s) if sum_s else 0.0
174	
175	    # VI Source Necessity = min-source-cover size / listed (paper "minimum vertex cover for source nodes").
176	    cover_size = minimum_source_cover_size(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
177	    source_necessity = (cover_size / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
178	    # SECONDARY disclosure retained so nothing is lost: the old sole-supporter count (lower bound on cover).
179	    n_sole_supporter = necessary_source_count(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
180	
181	    # I / II debate-only
182	    one_sided: Optional[int] = None
183	    overconfident: Optional[int] = None
184	    if is_debate:
185	        st = list(stance or [])
186	        has_pro = any(
187	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "pro") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
188	            for i in range(total)
189	        )
190	        has_con = any(
191	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "con") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
192	            for i in range(total)
193	        )
194	        one_sided = 0 if (has_pro and has_con) else 1
195	        conf5 = _confidence_is_overconfident(
196	            answer_confidence, list(statement_confidence or [None] * total)
197	        )
198	        overconfident = 1 if (one_sided == 1 and conf5) else 0
199	
200	    return {
201	        "n_statements": total,
202	        "n_relevant_statements": n_relevant,
203	        "n_listed_sources": n_sources,
204	        # lower-better
205	        "one_sided": one_sided,
206	        "overconfident": overconfident,
207	        "uncited_sources_fraction": round(uncited_fraction, 4),
208	        "unsupported_statements_ratio": round(unsupported_ratio, 4),
209	        # higher-better
210	        "relevant_statements_ratio": round(relevant_ratio, 4),
211	        "source_necessity": round(source_necessity, 4),
212	        "source_necessity_cover_size": cover_size,
213	        "n_sole_supporter": n_sole_supporter,
214	        "citation_accuracy": round(citation_accuracy, 4),
215	        "citation_thoroughness": round(citation_thoroughness, 4),
216	        # provenance / honesty
217	        "scorer": "polaris-reimpl-of-deeptrace",
218	        "is_estimate": True,
219	        "judge_substitution": "kimi-k2.6 (paper uses GPT-5) — DISCLOSED",
220	        "source_necessity_interpretation": (
221	            "min-source-cover (greedy set cover over supported relevant statements) / n_listed_sources; "
222	            "n_sole_supporter is the retained sole-supporter lower bound"
223	        ),
224	    }
225

1	# DeepTRACE BENCHMARK-SCORER judge lock — v1
2	# I-deepfix-001 (GH #1344) WS-14 / fix I5 — the measurement-precondition judge lock.
3	#
4	# WHAT THIS IS
5	# ------------
6	# The DeepTRACE 8-metric scorer (scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py) builds a binary
7	# Factual-Support Matrix S[i][j] = "does listed source j support statement i?". That per-(statement,
8	# source) support DECISION is made by a JUDGE. This file locks WHICH judge makes that BENCHMARK
9	# decision, and under WHAT comparability policy a "#1 on DeepTRACE" claim is allowed.
10	#
11	# WHY IT IS SEPARATE FROM THE RUNTIME LOCK (closes Codex iter-2 novel P1)
12	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
13	# This BENCHMARK-scorer judge is a DISTINCT role from the RUNTIME faithfulness-gating judge in
14	# config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml (currently qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, chosen for runtime
15	# stability + two-family segregation + sovereignty — NOT the properties a COMPARABLE benchmark number
16	# needs). Benchmark scoring is measurement, not runtime gating. Locking them separately keeps the
17	# runtime pipeline's model choice from silently deciding how a leaderboard number is produced.
18	#
19	# MUTATION / SIGNATURE POLICY (LAW VI config; operator is the only signer)
20	# ------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	#   - This lock is INERT until an operator signs it (signature.signed: true + operator + commit).
22	#   - scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py FAILS LOUD (refuses to let any DeepTRACE
23	#     number be claimed) while this lock is unsigned, or while the locked judge lacks OpenRouter
24	#     provider headroom for the per-report statement-support call volume.
25	#   - Editing the model, comparability policy, or headroom floor requires a fresh operator signature.
26	
27	version: 1
28	schema_version: 1.0
29	
30	purpose: |
31	  Operator-signed benchmark-scorer judge lock for the DeepTRACE re-implementation. Distinct from the
32	  runtime faithfulness-gating judge lock. Gates whether a DeepTRACE number is claimable at all.
33	
34	distinct_from_runtime_lock: true
35	runtime_lock_ref: config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml
36	
37	# === the benchmark-scorer judge ===
38	# Recommended default is a HIGH-provider-availability open reasoner (availability is binding: the
39	# statement-support judge runs ~one call PER (statement, listed-source) support cell, so many calls
40	# per report — a low-provider model 429s and the support matrix comes back partial). The operator
41	# fills/confirms the model and SIGNS. Two-family segregation vs the RUNTIME generator still holds.
42	benchmark_judge:
43	  model_slug: moonshotai/kimi-k2.6
44	  family: moonshot
45	  provider_selection: highest_operational_provider_count
46	  role_description: |
47	    Builds the binary Factual-Support Matrix for the DeepTRACE scorer (statement x listed-source).
48	    Not a runtime gate — a measurement judge. It never edits the pipeline output; it only scores it.
49	
50	# === comparability policy (BINDING) ===
51	# "#1 on DeepTRACE" is claimable ONLY if POLARIS AND every competitor are scored by the SAME
52	# benchmark judge on the SAME task set. Pick exactly ONE honest path and disclose it:
53	#   paper_judge  — use the paper's judge policy (GPT-5-class) so the number is directly comparable
54	#                  to the paper's PUBLISHED competitor scores.
55	#   self_rescore — POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor report itself with THIS benchmark judge; a fair
56	#                  RELATIVE comparison whose ABSOLUTE numbers are disclosed as NOT the paper's numbers.
57	# NEVER compare a self_rescore POLARIS number against a paper-published competitor number.
58	comparability:
59	  policy: self_rescore
60	  competitors_rescored_with_same_judge: true
61	  absolute_numbers_disclosed_as_non_paper: true
62	  never_mix_self_and_paper_numbers: true
63	
64	# === provider / headroom preflight floor ===
65	# The statement-support call volume per report is large, so the locked judge must have real OpenRouter
66	# provider redundancy. The preflight counts OPERATIONAL endpoints for benchmark_judge.model_slug and
67	# fails loud below this floor.
68	provider_headroom:
69	  min_operational_provider_count: 8
70	  expected_statement_support_calls_per_report: 178
71	  rationale: |
72	    ~178 statement-support decisions per drb_72-class report under concurrency. Below ~8 operational
73	    providers the judge 429s and the Factual-Support Matrix returns partial -> a wrong DeepTRACE
74	    number. 8 is the measured floor at which kimi-k2.6 sustained the call burst without partials.
75	
76	# === honesty flags surfaced with every number ===
77	disclosure:
78	  is_reimplementation: true
79	  no_public_official_scorer: true
80	  judge_is_substitution_for_paper_gpt5: true
81	
82	# === operator signature (INERT until signed) ===
83	# Operator accepted in-session (voice/chat, "I accept") on 2026-07-04 UTC. The operator accepts:
84	#   (1) moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 as the benchmark-scorer judge;
85	#   (2) comparability policy = self_rescore — POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor with THIS same judge,
86	#       and absolute numbers are disclosed as NOT the paper's published GPT-5 numbers (never mixed);
87	#   (3) disclosure = this is POLARIS's re-implementation of DeepTRACE, not the paper's official scorer.
88	# Recorded by the assistant on the operator's explicit acceptance; provider-headroom (>=8 operational
89	# providers) is still enforced at preflight time against live OpenRouter before any number is claimed.
90	signature:
91	  signed: true
92	  operator: aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com
93	  signed_at_utc: 2026-07-04T03:51:03Z
94	  signed_commit: 8e9811bb5c447c30303651b67b4153c38807fc19

content.text: The scorer is pure math over matrices built upstream by an LLM judge. Let me find the end-to-end driver.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Pack a POLARIS report into the official DeepTRACE (answer-engine-eval) input schema:
3	one record per task = {id, Question, Output, S1..S10}. The official scorer
4	(third_party/answer-engine-eval/Venkit.et.al.2024/populate_scores.py) then scrapes each
5	S{i} via r.jina.ai, gpt-4o-extracts core statements, and judges (statement, source) support.
6	
7	THE 10-SOURCE CAP (code-confirmed: populate_scores.py uses `range(1,11)` x3; the body's `[N]`
8	markers are parsed by utils_misc.extract_citations as source indices N). A POLARIS report
9	cites hundreds of sources, so this packer reduces the body to a MINIMAL load-bearing set of
10	<=10 sources via the harness's OWN greedy_set_cover objective (DeepTRACE source-necessity #6),
11	and RENUMBERS the body citations to `[1..k]`, k<=10. Statements whose only sources are dropped
12	become uncited (honest — we do not invent support). The full keep-all corroboration set is NOT
13	listed here (it lives in POLARIS's own bibliography/sidecar, scored by DRB-II independence).
14	
15	INVARIANTS (eval integrity):
16	  - NEVER pre-fills S{i}_content (the official scorer scrapes it; pre-filling POLARIS's own
17	    spans would be the §-1.1 self-serving-eval shortcut — forbidden).
18	  - Output is the cleaned answer body (masthead/base64/gap-stub stripped via pack_drb2.strip_junk,
19	    [#ev:] provenance tokens removed, appendix dropped) — never POLARIS-internal meta.
20	  - Faithfulness untouched: this packs/renumbers existing citations; it judges nothing.
21	LAW VI: caps are env-overridable.
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import argparse
26	import json
27	import os
28	import re
29	import sys
30	from collections import defaultdict
31	from pathlib import Path
32	
33	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
34	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO / "scripts" / "dr_benchmark"))
35	import pack_drb2  # noqa: E402  (reuse strip_junk / answer_body)
36	
37	MAX_SOURCES = int(os.getenv("PACK_DEEPTRACE_MAX_SOURCES", "10"))  # the official range(1,11) cap
38	
39	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[([\d,\s]+)\]")          # [N] / [N, M] numbered citations
40	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")      # POLARIS-internal provenance tokens (stripped)
41	# Intra-line sentence boundary (applied AFTER the body is split into lines): POLARIS's
42	# trailing-citation prose style `... end.[1] Next ...[2]`. Fixed-width lookbehind (1-char class).
43	_INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?\]])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])")
44	
45	
46	def _split_statements(body: str) -> list[str]:
47	    """Split a report body into the FINEST citation-bearing units for the source-necessity
48	    matrix. Each Markdown LINE is its own block, so table rows / list items / headings / block
49	    quotes never collapse into one another (the Codex P1 class — first lists, then tables); a
50	    table row (`|`-led) is further split per-cell on `|` so two cells citing two sources stay
51	    distinct; a prose line is split into sentences on terminal punctuation.
52	
53	    DIRECTIONAL SAFETY: over-splitting is harmless for greedy set-cover (a source that uniquely
54	    covers any unit is still selected — it never DROPS a load-bearing source); only UNDER-splitting
55	    (collapsing distinct cited units) drops sources. This matrix is INTERNAL source-selection only;
56	    the official scorer re-extracts statements from `Output` itself, so granularity here never
57	    changes what the judge reads — it only makes the <=10 kept sources strictly more conservative."""
58	    statements: list[str] = []
59	    for line in body.splitlines():
60	        s = line.strip()
61	        if not s:
62	            continue
63	        units = [c for c in s.split("|") if c.strip()] if s.startswith("|") else [s]
64	        for unit in units:
65	            for seg in _INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE.split(unit):
66	                if seg.strip():
67	                    statements.append(seg)
68	    return statements
69	
70	
71	def _load_references(bibliography: list) -> dict[int, str]:
72	    """Map the citation number `num` -> source url from a POLARIS bibliography.json (a list of
73	    {num, url, ...} entries). Skips malformed entries (no num/url)."""
74	    refs: dict[int, str] = {}
75	    for entry in bibliography:
76	        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
77	            continue
78	        num, url = entry.get("num"), entry.get("url")
79	        if num is None or not url:
80	            continue
81	        try:
82	            refs[int(num)] = str(url)
83	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
84	            continue
85	    return refs
86	
87	
88	def _citation_matrix(sentences: list[str]) -> dict[int, set[int]]:
89	    """source_num -> set of sentence indices that cite it (the CITATION bipartite)."""
90	    m: dict[int, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
91	    for i, s in enumerate(sentences):
92	        for grp in _CITE_RE.findall(s):
93	            for tok in grp.split(","):
94	                tok = tok.strip()
95	                if tok.isdigit():
96	                    m[int(tok)].add(i)
97	    return m
98	
99	
100	def _greedy_set_cover(universe: set[int], subsets: dict[int, set[int]]) -> list[int]:
101	    """Greedy set cover (mirrors third_party/answer-engine-eval utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover —
102	    the SAME objective the DeepTRACE source-necessity metric scores). Returns the source nums in
103	    coverage-priority order; deterministic tie-break by (-new_coverage, num)."""
104	    covered: set[int] = set()
105	    cover: list[int] = []
106	    items = list(subsets.items())
107	    while covered != universe and items:
108	        num, subset = max(items, key=lambda kv: (len(kv[1] - covered), -kv[0]))
109	        gain = len(subset - covered)
110	        if gain == 0:
111	            break
112	        cover.append(num)
113	        covered |= subset
114	        items = [(n, s) for n, s in items if n != num]
115	    return cover
116	
117	
118	def _renumber_body(body: str, num_to_new: dict[int, int]) -> str:
119	    """Rewrite `[old]`/`[old, ...]` -> `[new]` for selected sources; DROP citations to
120	    non-selected sources (the statement becomes uncited — never invent support)."""
121	    def repl(m: re.Match) -> str:
122	        new = sorted({num_to_new[int(t.strip())]
123	                      for t in m.group(1).split(",")
124	                      if t.strip().isdigit() and int(t.strip()) in num_to_new})
125	        return "[" + ", ".join(str(n) for n in new) + "]" if new else ""
126	    return _CITE_RE.sub(repl, body)
127	
128	
129	def pack_report_for_deeptrace(
130	    report_md: str, bibliography: list, *, task_id: object, question: str,
131	    max_sources: int = MAX_SOURCES,
132	) -> dict:
133	    """Return one DeepTRACE record {id, Question, Output, S1..S10} for a POLARIS report."""
134	    refs = _load_references(bibliography)
135	    # clean body: strip masthead/base64/gap-stub + appendix + provenance tokens.
136	    cleaned, _ = pack_drb2.strip_junk(report_md)
137	    body = pack_drb2.answer_body(cleaned)
138	    body = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", body)
139	    sentences = _split_statements(body)
140	    matrix = {n: idxs for n, idxs in _citation_matrix(sentences).items() if n in refs}
141	    universe = set().union(*matrix.values()) if matrix else set()
142	    # Clamp the selectable cap to 1..10 — the official scorer dereferences exactly S1..S10
143	    # (range(1,11)); a cap <10 would KeyError, >10 would emit out-of-range sources (Codex P1).
144	    n_cap = max(1, min(10, max_sources))
145	    cover = _greedy_set_cover(universe, matrix)[:n_cap]
146	    num_to_new = {old: i + 1 for i, old in enumerate(cover)}
147	    out_body = _renumber_body(body, num_to_new)
148	    record = {"id": str(task_id), "Question": question, "Output": out_body}
149	    for i in range(1, 11):  # ALWAYS emit exactly S1..S10 (empty string when unfilled).
150	        record[f"S{i}"] = refs.get(cover[i - 1], "") if i - 1 < len(cover) else ""
151	        # NB: S{i}_content is DELIBERATELY NOT set — the official scorer scrapes it via jina.
152	    return record
153	
154	
155	def main() -> None:
156	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Pack a POLARIS report for DeepTRACE (answer-engine-eval).")
157	    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True)
158	    ap.add_argument("--bibliography", required=True)
159	    ap.add_argument("--task-id", required=True)
160	    ap.add_argument("--question", required=True)
161	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True, help="output JSON path (a list of one record, appended)")
162	    args = ap.parse_args()
163	    bib = json.loads(Path(args.bibliography).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
164	    rec = pack_report_for_deeptrace(
165	        Path(args.report).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"), bib,
166	        task_id=args.task_id, question=args.question,
167	    )
168	    out = Path(args.out)
169	    data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if out.is_file() else []
170	    data = [d for d in data if d.get("id") != rec["id"]] + [rec]
171	    out.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
172	    # The record ALWAYS has exactly S1..S10 (the helper clamps the cap to 1..10); count over the
173	    # real schema, NOT the raw env cap — PACK_DEEPTRACE_MAX_SOURCES=99 would KeyError on S11 (Codex P1).
174	    n_src = sum(1 for i in range(1, 11) if rec[f"S{i}"])
175	    print(f"[pack_deeptrace] wrote {out} | id={rec['id']} | listed_sources={n_src} (<= 10)")
176	
177	
178	if __name__ == "__main__":
179	    main()
180

1	"""Path-B (system, question) scorer (I-safety-002b #925 PR-3).
2	
3	Loads a RECONCILED dual-§-1.1 audit ledger + the frozen-rubric JSON snapshot, runs the
4	existing two-lane scorer, and writes a per-(system, question) scored JSON.
5	
6	POLARIS-specific gate enforcement (Codex PR-3 design + Codex PR-2 P2 #2):
7	- If `<run_dir>/pathB_gate_INVALID` exists, refuse to score (the gate marked the run invalid).
8	- If `<run_dir>/pathB_gate_result.json` is missing OR verdict != "PASS", refuse to score.
9	- The gate is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for run validity; per-run artifacts (manifest, judge, etc.)
10	  may exist on disk even on FAIL, so the scorer MUST consult the sentinel + result first.
11	
12	CLI:
13	    python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.score_run \
14	        --system polaris --question Q75 \
15	        --rubric outputs/dr_benchmark/rubric_v3_frozen.json \
16	        --ledger outputs/dr_benchmark/ledgers/polaris_Q75_reconciled.json \
17	        --run-dir outputs/honest_sweep_r3/health/Q75 \
18	        --out outputs/dr_benchmark/scored/polaris_Q75.json
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import argparse
24	import json
25	import os
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	from scripts.dr_benchmark.ledger_schema import load_ledger
30	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.pathB_capture import corpus_truncated_from_manifest
31	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import (
32	    ClaimRow,
33	    RubricElement,
34	    SystemQuestionLedger,
35	    aggregate,
36	    system_passes_question,
37	)
38	
39	
40	class InvalidRunError(RuntimeError):
41	    """POLARIS run was marked INVALID by the Path-B gate. Refuse to score."""
42	
43	
44	def _read_polaris_gate_identity(run_dir: Path) -> dict:
45	    """Codex PR-3 diff P2 #2: surface the gate's identity pins (served provider+model per
46	    role + reachability_checked) for the aggregator's REQUIRED identity-pins block."""
47	    pin = json.loads((run_dir / "pathB_gate_pin.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
48	    result = json.loads((run_dir / "pathB_gate_result.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
49	    role_pins = pin.get("role_pins", [])
50	    return {
51	        "served_identity_by_role": result.get("served_identity_by_role", {}),
52	        "pinned_roles": [
53	            {"role": rp.get("role"), "model_slug": rp.get("model_slug"),
54	             "provider_name": rp.get("provider_name")}
55	            for rp in role_pins
56	        ],
57	        "reachability_checked": pin.get("reachability_checked"),
58	        "openrouter_allow_fallbacks": pin.get("openrouter_allow_fallbacks"),
59	        "openrouter_provider_order": pin.get("openrouter_provider_order"),
60	    }
61	
62	
63	def _check_polaris_gate(run_dir: Path) -> None:
64	    """For POLARIS scoring: the gate must have written a PASS result + no INVALID sentinel."""
65	    if not run_dir.exists():
66	        raise InvalidRunError(f"polaris run_dir does not exist: {run_dir}")
67	    # #958 (S2): scoring backstop — a truncated corpus is partial/invalid even if
68	    # the Path-B gate somehow recorded PASS. Read manifest.json directly.
69	    manifest_path = run_dir / "manifest.json"
70	    if manifest_path.exists():
71	        try:
72	            _manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
73	        except Exception:
74	            _manifest = {}
75	        if corpus_truncated_from_manifest(_manifest):
76	            _rt = _manifest.get("retrieval", {})
77	            raise InvalidRunError(
78	                f"polaris run_dir corpus truncated (partial corpus): "
79	                f"{_rt.get('candidates_processed')}/{_rt.get('candidates_total')} "
80	                f"candidates processed ({run_dir})"
81	            )
82	    if (run_dir / "pathB_gate_INVALID").exists():
83	        marker = (run_dir / "pathB_gate_INVALID").read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
84	        raise InvalidRunError(
85	            f"polaris run_dir marked INVALID by Path-B gate: {marker} ({run_dir})"
86	        )
87	    result_path = run_dir / "pathB_gate_result.json"
88	    if not result_path.exists():
89	        raise InvalidRunError(
90	            f"polaris run_dir missing pathB_gate_result.json (gate not run): {run_dir}"
91	        )
92	    result = json.loads(result_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
93	    if result.get("verdict") != "PASS":
94	        raise InvalidRunError(
95	            f"polaris run_dir gate verdict != PASS: {result.get('verdict')!r} "
96	            f"reason={result.get('reason')!r}"
97	        )
98	
99	
100	def score_one(
101	    *, system: str, question_id: str, rubric_path: Path, ledger_path: Path,
102	    run_dir: Path | None = None,
103	) -> dict:
104	    """Score one (system, question). Returns the scored dict; pure (no I/O beyond reads)."""
105	    polaris_gate_identity: dict | None = None
106	    if system == "polaris":
107	        if run_dir is None:
108	            raise InvalidRunError("polaris scoring requires --run-dir")
109	        _check_polaris_gate(run_dir)
110	        # Codex PR-3 diff P2 #2: surface the gate's served-identity + reachability for the
111	        # aggregator's REQUIRED identity-pins block (design answer F).
112	        polaris_gate_identity = _read_polaris_gate_identity(run_dir)
113	
114	    ledger = load_ledger(ledger_path)
115	    # Codex PR-3 diff P1 #1: require a RECONCILED ledger; single-auditor ledgers must not
116	    # be scored directly (that would bypass the dual-§-1.1 conservative-MAX discipline).
117	    if ledger.auditor != "reconciled":
118	        raise ValueError(
119	            f"ledger.auditor must be 'reconciled' (Claude+Codex dual audit), got "
120	            f"{ledger.auditor!r}; pass the reconciled ledger from "
121	            f"scripts.dr_benchmark.reconcile, not a single-auditor ledger"
122	        )
123	    if ledger.system != system:
124	        raise ValueError(
125	            f"ledger.system {ledger.system!r} != requested system {system!r}"
126	        )
127	    if ledger.question_id != question_id:
128	        raise ValueError(
129	            f"ledger.question_id {ledger.question_id!r} != requested {question_id!r}"
130	        )
131	
132	    rubric_doc = json.loads(rubric_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
133	    question_block = next(
134	        (q for q in rubric_doc["questions"] if q["question_id"] == question_id), None,
135	    )
136	    if question_block is None:
137	        raise ValueError(f"rubric does not contain question_id={question_id!r}")
138	    if rubric_doc.get("rubric_sha256") != ledger.rubric_sha256:
139	        raise ValueError(
140	            "rubric_sha256 mismatch — ledger was audited against a DIFFERENT pinned rubric: "
141	            f"ledger={ledger.rubric_sha256}, rubric_doc={rubric_doc.get('rubric_sha256')}"
142	        )
143	
144	    # Build ClaimRow[] from ledger.claims (mirrors validation; ledger already validated).
145	    rows = [
146	        ClaimRow(
147	            claim_id=c.claim_id,
148	            severity=c.severity,
149	            verdict=c.verdict,
150	            citation_id=c.citation_id,
151	            span_quote=c.span_quote,
152	            unreachable_subtype=c.unreachable_subtype,
153	            audit_note=c.audit_note,
154	        )
155	        for c in ledger.claims
156	    ]
157	    # Map coverage rows to RubricElement[] (the scorer expects covered+citation_supported).
158	    coverage_by_eid = {cv.element_id: cv for cv in ledger.coverage}
159	    rubric_elements = []
160	    for el in question_block["elements"]:
161	        cv = coverage_by_eid.get(el["element_id"])
162	        if cv is None:
163	            # No coverage row for a required element = NOT covered (conservative).
164	            rubric_elements.append(RubricElement(
165	                element_id=el["element_id"], covered=False, citation_supported=False,
166	            ))
167	        else:
168	            rubric_elements.append(RubricElement(
169	                element_id=el["element_id"],
170	                covered=cv.covered,
171	                citation_supported=cv.citation_supported,
172	            ))
173	
174	    result = system_passes_question(rows, rubric_elements)
175	    out = {
176	        "system": system,
177	        "question_id": question_id,
178	        "rubric_sha256": rubric_doc["rubric_sha256"],
179	        "ledger_audit_method": ledger.audit_method,
180	        "n_claims_material": result["lane1"]["material_atoms"],
181	        "n_rubric_elements": len(rubric_elements),
182	        "passed": result["passed"],
183	        "reasons": result["reasons"],
184	        "lane1": result["lane1"],
185	        "lane2": result["lane2"],
186	    }
187	    # I-perm-024 (#1216): wire the extended claim-by-claim metrics into the LIVE scorer
188	    # (the wiring audit found run_scorecard.py is never invoked in the live path — this is).
189	    # Default-OFF PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS -> byte-identical when off. Every metric is derived
190	    # ONLY from the audited ledger rows + the frozen rubric, NEVER raw report text (§-1.1). The
191	    # Claimify dedup is intentionally SKIPPED: the reconciled ledger's claims are already atomic
192	    # and unique by claim_id (the dual-§-1.1 audit deduplicates), so there is no inflation vector
193	    # to collapse and the ledger carries no per-claim text to dedup on.
194	    if os.environ.get("PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS", "").strip().lower() in (
195	        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
196	    ):
197	        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.extended_metrics import (
198	            citation_support_rate,
199	            diversity_score,
200	            faithfulness_precision,
201	            load_safety_floor_element_ids,
202	            required_entity_recall,
203	            safety_floor_recall,
204	        )
205	        _safety_ids = load_safety_floor_element_ids(question_id)
206	        out["extended"] = {
207	            "faithfulness_precision": faithfulness_precision(rows),
208	            "citation_support_rate": citation_support_rate(rows),
209	            "diversity_score": diversity_score(rows),
210	            "required_entity_recall": required_entity_recall(rubric_elements),
211	            "safety_floor_recall": safety_floor_recall(rubric_elements, _safety_ids),
212	            "dedup_applied": False,
213	            "methodology_note": (
214	                "derived from the reconciled dual-§-1.1 audit ledger rows (already atomic/"
215	                "unique by claim_id) + the frozen rubric; NEVER from raw report text; "
216	                "diversity_score is a diagnostic, NOT a superiority signal."
217	            ),
218	        }
219	    if polaris_gate_identity is not None:
220	        out["pathB_gate_identity"] = polaris_gate_identity
221	    return out
222	
223	
224	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
225	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Path-B (system, question) scorer.")
226	    p.add_argument("--system", required=True, choices=("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini"))
227	    p.add_argument("--question", required=True, dest="question_id",
228	                   choices=("Q72", "Q75", "Q76", "Q78", "Q90"))
229	    p.add_argument("--rubric", required=True, type=Path,
230	                   help="frozen-rubric JSON snapshot (rubric_v3_frozen.json)")
231	    p.add_argument("--ledger", required=True, type=Path,
232	                   help="reconciled dual-audit ledger JSON (claude+codex)")
233	    p.add_argument("--run-dir", type=Path, default=None,
234	                   help="polaris run dir (required for system=polaris; gate-check)")
235	    p.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path,
236	                   help="path to write scored JSON")
237	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
238	
239	    try:
240	        scored = score_one(
241	            system=args.system, question_id=args.question_id,
242	            rubric_path=args.rubric, ledger_path=args.ledger,
243	            run_dir=args.run_dir,
244	        )
245	    except InvalidRunError as exc:
246	        print(f"[score_run] INVALID: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
247	        # Write a stub INVALID record so the aggregator can still account for it.
248	        args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
249	        args.out.write_text(json.dumps({
250	            "system": args.system, "question_id": args.question_id,
251	            "passed": False, "invalid": True, "reason": str(exc),
252	        }, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
253	        return 2  # distinct from generic error
254	    except Exception as exc:
255	        print(f"[score_run] ERROR: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
256	        return 1
257	
258	    args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
259	    args.out.write_text(
260	        json.dumps(scored, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
261	        encoding="utf-8",
262	    )
263	    return 0
264	
265	
266	if __name__ == "__main__":
267	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-wire-007 (#1321) — ADAPTIVE AIMD controller ISOLATION sweep (VM-only).
3	
4	Proves the per-role AIMD concurrency controller (`adaptive_concurrency.py`, wired into
5	`openrouter_role_transport.complete()` for the sentinel role) on a LARGE claim set:
6	  - it RAMPS UP (probes +1 per clean window) from MIN toward the live provider ceiling,
7	  - BACKS OFF (halves) on a 429/503 or a force-close timeout,
8	  - SETTLES just under the discovered ceiling,
9	WITHOUT a 429 storm, with faithfulness UNCHANGED (every fabricated row still UNGROUNDED).
10	
11	Telemetry-ONLY harness (no change to any gated src file): a background sampler thread snapshots the
12	LIVE controller's `.limit` / `.in_flight` every `--sample-s` seconds, reconstructing the AIMD
13	trajectory. Back-off events are inferred from limit DROPS in the series; 429/503 from the transport's
14	own `rate_limit_counter_snapshot()`; force-closes from per-claim `parsed_ok=False`.
15	
16	Arms (set via env BEFORE importing the transport so the lazily-built controller reads them):
17	  - baseline  : PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_CONCURRENCY=0, static --workers (default 6). The control.
18	  - adaptive  : PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_CONCURRENCY=1, --workers >> sentinel MAX so the CONTROLLER, not
19	                the thread pool, is the binding constraint. Sentinel MAX from the shipped default (12)
20	                or raised via PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_SENTINEL_MAX to force a DISCOVERED ceiling.
21	
22	Run ON THE VM only (issues live OpenRouter sentinel POSTs; minimax-m2 is remote-only):
23	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/d8_adaptive_sweep.py --fixture /root/d8_large.json \
24	        --arm adaptive --workers 40 --out /root/d8_iso/adaptive_max32.json
25	"""
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import argparse
29	import concurrent.futures as cf
30	import json
31	import os
32	import statistics
33	import sys
34	import threading
35	import time
36	from pathlib import Path
37	
38	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
39	if str(_REPO) not in sys.path:
40	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO))
41	
42	
43	def _set_arm_env(arm: str) -> None:
44	    """Set adaptive on/off BEFORE importing the transport (controller is lazily built on first
45	    acquire and reads these at construction). Faithfulness logic untouched — concurrency knobs only."""
46	    os.environ["PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT"] = "openrouter"
47	    if arm == "baseline":
48	        os.environ["PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_CONCURRENCY"] = "0"
49	    elif arm == "adaptive":
50	        os.environ["PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_CONCURRENCY"] = "1"
51	    else:
52	        raise SystemExit(f"unknown arm: {arm}")
53	
54	
55	def main() -> None:
56	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
57	    ap.add_argument("--fixture", required=True)
58	    ap.add_argument("--arm", required=True, choices=["baseline", "adaptive"])
59	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True)
60	    ap.add_argument("--workers", type=int, default=6)
61	    ap.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0, help="0 = all fixture rows")
62	    ap.add_argument("--sample-s", type=float, default=1.0, help="controller poll interval (s)")
63	    ap.add_argument("--total-claims", type=int, default=1220)
64	    args = ap.parse_args()
65	
66	    _set_arm_env(args.arm)
67	
68	    # Import AFTER env is set so the lazily-built controller honors the arm knobs.
69	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import EvidenceDocument  # noqa: PLC0415
70	    from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_adapter import run_sentinel  # noqa: PLC0415
71	    from src.polaris_graph.roles import openrouter_role_transport as _ort  # noqa: PLC0415
72	    from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b import build_gate_b_transport  # noqa: PLC0415
73	
74	    slug = os.environ.get("PG_SENTINEL_MODEL_SLUG", "minimax/minimax-m2")
75	    os.environ["BENCHMARK_VERIFIER_SENTINEL"] = slug
76	    # Keep the production-safe sentinel deadline (NOT the 30-45s over-drop trap).
77	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S_SENTINEL", "300")
78	
79	    rows = json.loads(Path(args.fixture).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
80	    if args.limit:
81	        rows = rows[: args.limit]
82	
83	    transport = build_gate_b_transport()
84	    _ort.reset_rate_limit_counter()
85	
86	    # --- controller-trajectory sampler (telemetry only; reads .limit/.in_flight) ------------------
87	    series: list[dict] = []
88	    stop = threading.Event()
89	    t0 = time.time()
90	
91	    def _sample_controller():
92	        return _ort._adaptive_controller("sentinel") if args.arm == "adaptive" else None
93	
94	    def _sampler():
95	        while not stop.is_set():
96	            c = _sample_controller()
97	            if c is not None:
98	                try:
99	                    series.append({
100	                        "t": round(time.time() - t0, 2),
101	                        "limit": c.limit,
102	                        "in_flight": c.in_flight,
103	                    })
104	                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
105	                    pass
106	            stop.wait(args.sample_s)
107	
108	    sampler = threading.Thread(target=_sampler, daemon=True)
109	    sampler.start()
110	
111	    def _evidence(span: str):
112	        return [EvidenceDocument(doc_id="iso", text=span)]
113	
114	    def _one(row: dict) -> dict:
115	        t = time.time()
116	        err = None
117	        try:
118	            result, _recs = run_sentinel(
119	                transport, row["claim"], _evidence(row["span"]),
120	                model_slug=slug, mode="decomposition",
121	            )
122	            verdict = result.verdict.name
123	            parsed_ok = bool(getattr(result, "parsed_ok", True))
124	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
125	            verdict, parsed_ok, err = "UNGROUNDED", False, f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
126	        dt = time.time() - t
127	        return {
128	            "id": row["id"], "label": row["label"], "kind": row.get("kind"),
129	            "verdict": verdict, "parsed_ok": parsed_ok, "latency_s": round(dt, 2), "error": err,
130	        }
131	
132	    results: list[dict] = []
133	    wall0 = time.time()
134	    with cf.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=args.workers) as pool:
135	        for r in pool.map(_one, rows):
136	            results.append(r)
137	            tag = "REAL" if r["parsed_ok"] else "DEGRADED"
138	            c = _sample_controller()
139	            lim = c.limit if c is not None else args.workers
140	            print(f"  [{tag}] lim={lim:2} {r['id'][:28]:28} {r['label']:10} -> {r['verdict']:11} "
141	                  f"{r['latency_s']:6.1f}s" + (f"  ERR {r['error'][:40]}" if r["error"] else ""),
142	                  flush=True)
143	    wall = time.time() - wall0
144	    stop.set()
145	    sampler.join(timeout=3)
146	
147	    # --- AIMD trajectory analysis from the limit series -------------------------------------------
148	    limits = [s["limit"] for s in series]
149	    backoff_events = sum(1 for a, b in zip(limits, limits[1:]) if b < a)  # limit DROPs == back-offs
150	    ramp_events = sum(1 for a, b in zip(limits, limits[1:]) if b > a)     # limit RISES == probe-ups
151	    settled_limit = statistics.mode(limits[len(limits) // 2:]) if len(limits) >= 4 else (limits[-1] if limits else args.workers)
152	    peak_limit = max(limits) if limits else args.workers
153	    start_limit = limits[0] if limits else args.workers
154	
155	    lats = [r["latency_s"] for r in results]
156	    real = [r for r in results if r["parsed_ok"]]
157	    fabs = [r for r in results if r["label"] == "fabricated"]
158	    fab_caught = [r for r in fabs if r["verdict"] == "UNGROUNDED"]
159	    grounded = [r for r in results if r["label"] == "grounded"]
160	    median_s = statistics.median(lats) if lats else 0.0
161	    p99_s = sorted(lats)[int(len(lats) * 0.99) - 1] if len(lats) >= 2 else (lats[0] if lats else 0.0)
162	    claims_per_min = (len(results) / wall * 60.0) if wall else 0.0
163	    # whole-D8 wall extrapolation: real throughput-based (claims/min), not the static-worker formula.
164	    extrap_min = (args.total_claims / claims_per_min) if claims_per_min else 0.0
165	
166	    rl = _ort.rate_limit_counter_snapshot()
167	
168	    summary = {
169	        "arm": args.arm, "n_claims": len(results), "workers": args.workers,
170	        "wall_s": round(wall, 1),
171	        "adaptive_controller": {
172	            "enabled": args.arm == "adaptive",
173	            "sentinel_min_max": [
174	                int(os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_SENTINEL_MIN", "4")),
175	                int(os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_ADAPTIVE_SENTINEL_MAX", "12")),
176	            ],
177	            "start_limit": start_limit, "peak_limit": peak_limit, "settled_limit": settled_limit,
178	            "ramp_events": ramp_events, "backoff_events": backoff_events,
179	            "n_samples": len(series),
180	        },
181	        "throughput": {
182	            "claims_per_min": round(claims_per_min, 2),
183	            "median_s_per_claim": round(median_s, 2),
184	            "p99_s_per_claim": round(p99_s, 2),
185	            "extrapolated_whole_D8_min_for_%d_claims" % args.total_claims: round(extrap_min, 1),
186	        },
187	        "completeness": {
188	            "pct_real_verdict": round(100.0 * len(real) / max(len(results), 1), 1),
189	            "n_real": len(real), "n_degraded": len(results) - len(real),
190	        },
191	        "faithfulness_catch": {
192	            "n_fabricated": len(fabs), "n_caught_ungrounded": len(fab_caught),
193	            "catch_rate": round(len(fab_caught) / max(len(fabs), 1), 4),
194	            "n_caught_via_real_decomposition": sum(
195	                1 for r in fabs if r["verdict"] == "UNGROUNDED" and r["parsed_ok"]),
196	            "n_caught_via_failclose_only": sum(
197	                1 for r in fabs if r["verdict"] == "UNGROUNDED" and not r["parsed_ok"]),
198	            "false_accepts": [r["id"] for r in fabs if r["verdict"] == "GROUNDED"],
199	            "n_grounded_overflag": sum(
200	                1 for r in grounded if r["verdict"] == "UNGROUNDED" and r["parsed_ok"]),
201	        },
202	        "rate_limit_429_503": rl,
203	        "limit_series": series,
204	        "latency_series_s": lats,
205	    }
206	    Path(args.out).write_text(json.dumps(summary, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
207	    print("\n=== SUMMARY", args.arm, "===")
208	    print(json.dumps({k: v for k, v in summary.items()
209	                      if k not in ("limit_series", "latency_series_s")}, indent=2))

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-deepfix-001 fix I5 — DeepTRACE benchmark-judge provider/headroom preflight (the measurement gate).
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (the measurement precondition, closes the Codex iter-2 novel P1)
5	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6	The DeepTRACE 8-metric scorer (``scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py``) needs a binary
7	Factual-Support Matrix ``S[i][j] = "does listed source j support statement i?"``. That per-(statement,
8	source) decision is made by a BENCHMARK-SCORER judge locked SEPARATELY from the runtime faithfulness
9	judge in ``config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml``. A drb_72-class report makes ~178 statement-
10	support calls under concurrency, so the locked judge MUST have real OpenRouter provider redundancy — a
11	low-provider model 429s and the support matrix comes back PARTIAL, which silently produces a WRONG
12	DeepTRACE number. And a number scored under an UNSIGNED lock is not an operator-authorized measurement.
13	
14	THIS PREFLIGHT FAILS LOUD (refuses to let any DeepTRACE number be claimed) when ANY of:
15	  1. the lock is UNSIGNED (``signature.signed`` is not true) — the lock is inert until the operator signs.
16	  2. the locked benchmark judge has FEWER operational OpenRouter providers than
17	     ``provider_headroom.min_operational_provider_count`` — the support matrix would return partial.
18	  3. provider headroom could NOT be measured (no ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` / network error) — never a
19	     silent pass; an unmeasured headroom is a blocker, not an OK.
20	  4. the comparability policy is missing or not one of the recognized honest paths — a number produced
21	     under an undeclared comparability policy is not comparable and must not be claimed.
22	
23	DNA: this is a MEASUREMENT gate + measurement-judge lock, not a pipeline knob. It has NO cap / target /
24	thinner on the pipeline; it only decides whether a DeepTRACE NUMBER is claimable. Fail-loud, never a
25	silent fallback (LAW II). Every threshold comes from the operator-signed lock (LAW VI).
26	
27	BUILD-ONLY / OFFLINE-TESTABLE
28	-----------------------------
29	``evaluate_preflight(lock, operational_provider_count)`` is a PURE function over a lock dict + an integer
30	count (offline, $0, no network) — that is what the unit test exercises RED->GREEN. Only ``--check`` touches
31	the network (a single GET to OpenRouter ``/models/{slug}/endpoints`` to count operational providers, using
32	the SAME operational definition as ``scripts/diagnostics/rank_openrouter_providers.py``: ``status == 0``
33	AND ``uptime_last_30m >= PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR``).
34	
35	Usage
36	-----
37	    # offline: evaluate the lock with a known provider count (e.g. from a prior --check)
38	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --provider-count 21
39	
40	    # live: count operational providers for the locked judge, then evaluate (exit 0 clean, 4 == blocked)
41	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check
42	"""
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import argparse
46	import json
47	import os
48	import sys
49	import urllib.request
50	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
51	from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional
52	
53	# The default DeepTRACE benchmark-judge lock path (LAW VI: overridable via --lock).
54	DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH = "config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml"
55	
56	# Operational-endpoint definition, IDENTICAL to scripts/diagnostics/rank_openrouter_providers.py so the
57	# headroom count matches the routing layer's notion of "healthy". LAW VI: env-overridable floor.
58	_UPTIME_FLOOR = float(os.getenv("PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR", "98.0"))
59	
60	# The honest comparability policies the lock may declare (arXiv 2509.04499 measurement discipline).
61	_VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES = {"paper_judge", "self_rescore"}
62	
63	
64	@dataclass
65	class PreflightResult:
66	    """The outcome of the DeepTRACE benchmark-judge preflight. ``claimable`` is True IFF a DeepTRACE
67	    number may be claimed (lock signed, provider headroom met + measured, comparability declared)."""
68	
69	    claimable: bool
70	    blockers: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
71	    signed: bool = False
72	    model_slug: Optional[str] = None
73	    min_provider_floor: Optional[int] = None
74	    operational_provider_count: Optional[int] = None
75	    comparability_policy: Optional[str] = None
76	
77	
78	def load_lock(path: str = DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH) -> dict:
79	    """Load the benchmark-judge lock YAML. Raises FileNotFoundError (fail-loud) if it is missing — a
80	    DeepTRACE number cannot be claimed without the operator-signed lock present."""
81	    import yaml  # local import: keep the module import lightweight for the pure path
82	
83	    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
84	        data = yaml.safe_load(fh)
85	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
86	        raise ValueError(f"{path}: expected a YAML mapping, got {type(data).__name__}")
87	    return data
88	
89	
90	def _as_bool(value: Any) -> bool:
91	    if isinstance(value, bool):
92	        return value
93	    return str(value).strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
94	
95	
96	def evaluate_preflight(
97	    lock: Mapping[str, Any],
98	    operational_provider_count: Optional[int],
99	) -> PreflightResult:
100	    """PURE preflight (offline, $0). Decide whether a DeepTRACE number is claimable for the given lock +
101	    measured operational provider count. ``operational_provider_count is None`` means "could not measure"
102	    and is itself a blocker (never a silent pass).
103	
104	    Blockers (fail-loud, LAW II):
105	      * lock unsigned;
106	      * comparability policy missing / unrecognized;
107	      * provider headroom unmeasured (count is None);
108	      * operational provider count below the lock's ``min_operational_provider_count`` floor.
109	    """
110	    blockers: list[str] = []
111	
112	    signature = lock.get("signature") or {}
113	    signed = _as_bool(signature.get("signed", False))
114	    if not signed:
115	        blockers.append(
116	            "benchmark-judge lock is UNSIGNED (signature.signed is not true) — the lock is INERT until "
117	            "an operator signs it; NO DeepTRACE number is claimable. Sign "
118	            f"{DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH} (signature.signed:true + operator + signed_commit)."
119	        )
120	
121	    benchmark_judge = lock.get("benchmark_judge") or {}
122	    model_slug = benchmark_judge.get("model_slug")
123	    if not model_slug:
124	        blockers.append("benchmark_judge.model_slug is empty — the benchmark judge is not declared.")
125	
126	    comparability = lock.get("comparability") or {}
127	    policy = comparability.get("policy")
128	    if policy not in _VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES:
129	        blockers.append(
130	            f"comparability.policy={policy!r} is not one of {sorted(_VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES)} — a "
131	            "DeepTRACE number produced under an undeclared comparability policy is not comparable and "
132	            "must not be claimed (never mix self_rescore POLARIS numbers with paper-published numbers)."
133	        )
134	
135	    headroom = lock.get("provider_headroom") or {}
136	    try:
137	        min_floor = int(headroom.get("min_operational_provider_count"))
138	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
139	        min_floor = None
140	    if min_floor is None:
141	        blockers.append(
142	            "provider_headroom.min_operational_provider_count is missing/invalid — the headroom floor "
143	            "is undefined, so headroom cannot be enforced."
144	        )
145	
146	    if operational_provider_count is None:
147	        blockers.append(
148	            "provider headroom could NOT be measured (no OPENROUTER_API_KEY or a network error on the "
149	            "/models/{slug}/endpoints probe) — an UNMEASURED headroom is a blocker, never a silent pass. "
150	            "Run --check with OPENROUTER_API_KEY set."
151	        )
152	    elif min_floor is not None and operational_provider_count < min_floor:
153	        blockers.append(
154	            f"benchmark judge {model_slug!r} has {operational_provider_count} operational OpenRouter "
155	            f"provider(s) < the lock floor of {min_floor} — the ~"
156	            f"{headroom.get('expected_statement_support_calls_per_report', '?')} statement-support "
157	            "calls/report would 429 and the Factual-Support Matrix would return PARTIAL -> a wrong "
158	            "DeepTRACE number. Raise headroom (a higher-provider judge) or lower the operator-signed "
159	            "floor before claiming a number."
160	        )
161	
162	    return PreflightResult(
163	        claimable=not blockers,
164	        blockers=blockers,
165	        signed=signed,
166	        model_slug=model_slug,
167	        min_provider_floor=min_floor,
168	        operational_provider_count=operational_provider_count,
169	        comparability_policy=policy,
170	    )
171	
172	
173	def count_operational_providers(model_slug: str, timeout: float = 30.0) -> Optional[int]:
174	    """Count OpenRouter endpoints for ``model_slug`` that are OPERATIONAL (status == 0 AND
175	    uptime_last_30m >= PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR). Returns None on any network/auth failure (the caller
176	    treats None as a fail-loud blocker — never a silent 0-or-pass). Network; only called by --check."""
177	    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
178	    if not api_key:
179	        return None
180	    url = f"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models/{model_slug}/endpoints"
181	    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}"})
182	    try:
183	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
184	            payload = json.loads(resp.read())
185	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — any probe failure => unmeasured (None), a fail-loud blocker upstream
186	        return None
187	    endpoints = (payload.get("data") or {}).get("endpoints") or []
188	    operational = 0
189	    for ep in endpoints:
190	        up = ep.get("uptime_last_30m")
191	        if ep.get("status") == 0 and (up is None or up >= _UPTIME_FLOOR):
192	            operational += 1
193	    return operational
194	
195	
196	def _print_result(result: PreflightResult) -> None:
197	    print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] benchmark judge:", result.model_slug)
198	    print(f"[deeptrace-judge-preflight] lock signed: {result.signed}")
199	    print(
200	        "[deeptrace-judge-preflight] operational providers:",
201	        result.operational_provider_count,
202	        f"(floor {result.min_provider_floor})",
203	    )
204	    print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] comparability policy:", result.comparability_policy)
205	    if result.claimable:
206	        print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] OK — a DeepTRACE number is claimable under this lock.")
207	    else:
208	        print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] BLOCKED — a DeepTRACE number is NOT claimable:")
209	        for b in result.blockers:
210	            print(f"  - {b}")
211	
212	
213	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
214	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
215	        description="DeepTRACE benchmark-judge provider/headroom preflight (I5 measurement gate)."
216	    )
217	    ap.add_argument("--lock", default=DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH, help="path to the benchmark-judge lock YAML")
218	    ap.add_argument(
219	        "--check",
220	        action="store_true",
221	        help="LIVE: count operational OpenRouter providers for the locked judge, then evaluate",
222	    )
223	    ap.add_argument(
224	        "--provider-count",
225	        type=int,
226	        default=None,
227	        help="offline: evaluate against a KNOWN operational-provider count (skips the network probe)",
228	    )
229	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
230	
231	    try:
232	        lock = load_lock(args.lock)
233	    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as exc:
234	        print(f"[deeptrace-judge-preflight] BLOCKED — cannot load lock: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
235	        return 4
236	
237	    count: Optional[int] = args.provider_count
238	    if args.check:
239	        model_slug = (lock.get("benchmark_judge") or {}).get("model_slug") or ""
240	        count = count_operational_providers(model_slug) if model_slug else None
241	
242	    result = evaluate_preflight(lock, count)
243	    _print_result(result)
244	    return 0 if result.claimable else 4
245	
246	
247	if __name__ == "__main__":
248	    sys.exit(main())
249

content.text:
I now have a complete, verified picture. Let me record the honest result.

Verified facts:
- The DeepTRACE scorer math (`deeptrace_scorer.py`) is real and runs (confirmed on a synthetic matrix: citation_accuracy/thoroughness/etc. all compute).
- But it needs a Factual-Support matrix S built by a judge, and that path is not runnable here.

The blocks I confirmed by execution:
1. `deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check` returns **BLOCKED** (exit 4): `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is unset → provider headroom unmeasurable → per the operator-signed lock, no DeepTRACE number is claimable.
